You can use the Pylon API to programmatically access and take action on data within Pylon. Common use cases include fetching information about customers, fetching information about issues, and creating issues programmatically.\\\\\\\"\\n\\n**Authentication**\\n- **URL:** https://docs.usepylon.com/pylon-docs/developer/api/authentication\\n- **How it works:** \\\\\\\"The Pylon API uses Bearer authentication. This means you’ll need to pass your API token in the header of your request: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`\\\\\\\"\\n- **Token Type:** Bearer Token.\\n\\n**Token Creation**\\n- **URL:** https://docs.usepylon.com/pylon-docs/developer/api\\n- **Who can create tokens:** \\\\\\\"Only Admin users can create API tokens.\\\\\\\"\\n\\n**Rate Limits**\\n- **Status:** No specific rate limit notes, throttling policies, or request limits were found on the main API, Authentication, Errors, or API Reference pages.\",\...[truncated]

raw_result.description: AI-native B2B support platform across every channel.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs state the API uses Bearer authentication via an API token in the Authorization header; only Admin users can create API tokens in the Pylon dashboard.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public pricing is listed for paid Starter, Professional, and Enterprise plans; API token creation is restricted to Admin users within a Pylon account.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer docs describe a fairly broad REST API covering Accounts, Activities, Attachments, Audit Logs, Call Recordings, Contacts, Custom Fields, Custom Objects, Feature Requests, Issues, Knowledge Base, Macros, Me, Messages, Surveys, Tags, Tasks & Projects, Teams, Ticket Forms, Training Data, User roles, and Users.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No MCP server or registry entry was verified from the gathered evidence. This remains unconfirmed rather than disproven.

raw_result.blocker: API access appears buildable from public docs, but token creation requires an Admin user inside a paid Pylon account.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No specific rate limit or throttling documentation was found in the gathered official API, authentication, errors, or API reference pages.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official public docs say the API supports read/write access and common use cases like fetching customers, fetching issues, and creating issues programmatically. Public pricing is self-serve-visible, and the API reference covers support entities plus admin metadata, suggesting meaningful toolkit surface area.
raw_result.human_review_reason: MCP availability was not verified from primary evidence, and the exact self-serve API eligibility beyond requiring a paid account plus Admin-created token is somewhat ambiguous from the gathered pages.
